questions.q: Why did Krishna found the city of Dwarka?
options: To be nearer the plains of Kurukshetra
To fulfil a vow made to the sea god
To move the Yadavas beyond the reach of Jarasandha's sieges
To rule jointly with the Pandava brothers
questions.explanation: Jarasandha's repeated assaults made Mathura unlivable, so Krishna shifted the Yadavas to a new fortress city on the distant western coast.

questions.q: Which divine architect built Dwarka?
questions.explanation: Vishvakarma, architect of the gods, raised the golden city of Dwarka at Krishna's behest.

questions.q: On which shore did Dwarka rise?
options: The edge of the western ocean
questions.explanation: Dwarka was built on the western seacoast, far from the Gangetic heartland of the Kurus.

questions.q: What was the fate of Dwarka after Krishna's death?
options: It was swallowed by the sea
It was sacked by Jarasandha's heirs
It crumbled in a great earthquake
questions.explanation: Exactly as foretold, the ocean rose and engulfed Dwarka after Krishna left the world.

questions.q: How many days after Krishna's passing did the sea claim Dwarka?
questions.explanation: The Mausala Parva records that the sea swallowed the city on the seventh day after Krishna's death.

questions.q: Which clan ruled from Dwarka during its golden age?
questions.explanation: Dwarka served as the capital of the Yadavas, the clan of Krishna and Balarama.

questions.q: What does the name Dwarka evoke?
questions.explanation: The name comes from dvara, meaning gate or door, marking it as the many-gated city.

questions.q: From which besieged city did Krishna lead his people to Dwarka?
questions.explanation: Krishna evacuated the Yadavas from Mathura, which Jarasandha had attacked again and again.

questions.q: At Prabhasa near Dwarka, how did Krishna meet his end?
options: He fell in the drunken Yadava brawl
He walked into the rising sea
He gave up his body in a sacred fire
A hunter's arrow pierced his heel
questions.explanation: The hunter Jara mistook Krishna's foot for a deer and shot the arrow that ended his earthly life.

questions.q: In which modern Indian state does tradition place ancient Dwarka?
questions.explanation: Dwarka is identified with the coastal town of the same name in Gujarat, where marine archaeology has explored submerged ruins.

questions.q: Why did Jarasandha besiege Mathura again and again?
options: Krishna had stolen the Syamantaka jewel from him
Krishna had slain Kamsa, the husband of Jarasandha's daughters
The Yadavas had raided his capital Girivraja
Balarama had insulted him at a royal swayamvara
questions.explanation: Kamsa was Jarasandha's son-in-law, and the Magadhan emperor swore vengeance when Krishna killed him.

questions.q: Who sat as the crowned king of the Yadavas at Dwarka?
questions.explanation: Krishna restored Ugrasena to the throne after slaying Kamsa, and Ugrasena reigned as the formal king at Dwarka.

questions.q: On which festive hill near Dwarka did Arjuna first behold Subhadra?
questions.explanation: During a great festival on Mount Raivataka, Arjuna saw Subhadra and resolved to win her.

questions.q: Who led Dwarka's survivors away before the sea came?
questions.explanation: Arjuna travelled to Dwarka after the Yadava massacre and led the women, children, and elders out as the waters began to rise.

questions.q: Where was young Vajra enthroned after the exodus from Dwarka?
questions.explanation: The Mausala Parva says Arjuna installed Vajra as king at Indraprastha, though some later retellings place him at Mathura.

questions.q: Who attacked the refugee column on the road from Dwarka?
questions.explanation: Abhira robbers fell upon the caravan, and Arjuna, his divine prowess faded, could not protect many of Krishna's women.

questions.q: What destroyed the Yadava men shortly before the sea took Dwarka?
options: A plague sent by angered sages
A drunken brawl fought with iron-hard eraka grass
Shalva's renewed assault from the sky
A famine that emptied the city
questions.explanation: At Prabhasa the drunken Yadavas turned on one another, and the eraka reeds they snatched up became iron in their hands.

questions.q: Which grieving queen's curse foretold the destruction of Krishna's clan and capital?
questions.explanation: After her hundred sons died, Gandhari cursed Krishna that the Yadavas would slaughter one another in thirty-six years.

questions.q: Who was dressed as a pregnant woman in the prank that drew the sages' curse on the Yadavas?
questions.explanation: The Yadava youths presented Krishna's son Samba as a pregnant woman, and the insulted sages decreed he would deliver an iron pestle to destroy the clan.

questions.q: What was done with the cursed iron pestle after it was ground to powder?
options: It was buried beneath the palace floor
It was melted into a temple bell
It was cast into the sea, washing ashore as deadly grass
It was hurled into a mountain chasm
questions.explanation: The powder thrown into the sea returned on the shore of Prabhasa as razor-sharp eraka grass, the very weapon of the Yadava self-slaughter.

questions.q: By what alternative name was Krishna's sea-girt capital known?
questions.explanation: The epic frequently calls the city Dvaravati, another form of the name Dwarka.

questions.q: Where did Krishna send the Yadavas for the seaside festival that became their last?
questions.explanation: Seeing evil omens fill Dwarka, Krishna directed the Yadavas to the holy shore of Prabhasa, where the fratricidal brawl erupted.

questions.q: How many times is Jarasandha commonly said to have attacked Mathura before the move to Dwarka?
questions.explanation: The Puranic count has Jarasandha defeated seventeen times, with the next campaign prompting Krishna to shift the Yadavas west.

questions.q: Which other invader menaced Mathura at the time of the move and was lured to his doom in Muchukunda's cave?
questions.explanation: Krishna led the foreign king Kalayavana into the cave where the sleeping Muchukunda's awakened gaze burned him to ash, a tale from the Harivamsha and Puranas.

questions.q: Which epithet did Krishna earn for quitting the battlefield of Mathura?
questions.explanation: Tradition, especially in Gujarat, lovingly calls Krishna Ranchhod, the one who left the battlefield, for choosing strategic retreat to Dwarka over pride.

questions.q: Dwarka rose on the site of which ancient city of King Revata's line?
questions.explanation: The new capital was raised on the site of Kushasthali, the old fortress of Raivata's dynasty in the Anarta country.

questions.q: How was Vajra, the last king sent out of Dwarka, related to Krishna?
questions.explanation: Vajra was the son of Aniruddha and grandson of Pradyumna, making him Krishna's great-grandson and the surviving heir of the Yadavas.

questions.q: Which parva of the Mahabharata narrates the destruction of Dwarka?
questions.explanation: The Mausala Parva, the book of the iron pestle, tells of the Yadava massacre, Krishna's death, and the sea taking Dwarka.

questions.q: What was the name of the hunter whose arrow killed Krishna near Dwarka?
questions.explanation: The hunter Jara, whose name means old age, mistook Krishna's heel for a deer and loosed the fatal arrow at Prabhasa.

questions.q: Who carried the news of the Yadava massacre from Dwarka to the Pandavas?
questions.explanation: Krishna's charioteer Daruka rode to Hastinapura to summon Arjuna for the evacuation of the doomed city.
